Interview Process:
The vetting process involves a technical interview conducted by a human; you will not be allowed to use an AI IDE (Integrated Development Environment) but you will be allowed to use LLMs or Stack Overflow

You must be able to commit around 20 hours per week for this role
This contract is expected to last at least 1 month

This role will pay $100/h based on experience

Today I am going to talk about how a virtual assistant help you 

In 2026 a virtual assistant is far more than just remote secretary. A virtual assistant strategic operators who use a mix of specialized skills to handle the heavy lifting of your daily operation. Whether you are an entrepreneur, a busy professional, or a small business owner, here is how a VA helps you

1/ Reclaiming Your Time : The 15-Hours 
The biggest benefit is time recovery. On average, professionals who delegate to a VA reclaim 10 to 15 hours per week. This allows you to shift from working in the business (busy work) to working on the business.

2/ Specialized Task Management
Modern VAs often specialize in specific niches. You can hire them to handle:  
A/ Administration: Email filtering, complex calendar scheduling, and travel logistics.  
B/ Digital Marketing: Managing social media feeds, scheduling posts, and basic SEO updates.  
C/ Sales Support: Lead generation, CRM management, and following up with prospects to close deals faster.  
4/ Operations: Invoicing, bookkeeping, and managing project boards like Asana or Trello

3/Cost Efficiency

Hiring a VA can reduce operational costs by up to 78% compared to a full-time, in-house employee.  
A/ No Overhead: You don't pay for office space, equipment, or benefits.  
B/ Scalability: You can hire them for 5 hours a week or 40, depending on your current needs, without the risk of long-term employment contract.

4/ The AI Operator Advantage
In 2026, top-tier VAs act as human-in-the-loop operators. They use AI tools (like ChatGPT, Zapier, or Midjourney) to produce work 3x faster than a traditional assistant could. They handle the nuance that AI misses—like understanding the emotional tone of a client’s email or double-checking AI-generated data for accuracy.

#virtualassistant #adminsupport #businesssales #planforbusiness #marketingexperat #digitalmarketingexpert

Penn Law built an AI bootcamp. Two weekends. Two credits. Students assigned as buyer’s counsel or seller’s counsel, triaging a large dataset of contracts with AI tools under real-world time pressure, identifying diligence red flags, and turning findings into client-ready outputs.

It was a simulated M&A due diligence sprint. The kind of exercise most associates do not encounter until their first live deal.

I spoke on a panel there last weekend. The students were not learning about AI. They were learning to supervise it. They could tell you where the tool saved time, where it needed a better prompt, and where the output required substantive legal judgment to fix.

Law schools are a leading indicator for the profession. When a top program decides something deserves dedicated course credit, it is telling you what the hiring market will expect in 18 months.

Medical schools did not wait for hospitals to mandate simulation training. They built it into the program because they understood the standard of care was moving. The hospitals followed.

Most AmLaw firms right now offer AI training that amounts to a lunch-and-learn and a PDF guide. A second-year law student at Penn has more structured AI instruction than the average fifth-year associate at a major firm.

That is not a criticism of the associate. It is a question for firm leadership. If your AI program fits in a conference room over lunch, it is not a program. It is a briefing.

The students entering the market in 2027 will expect their employers to be at least as sophisticated as their law school was. The firms that meet that expectation will get first pick. The ones that do not will be wondering why their offer acceptance rates are declining.

#LegalAI #BigLaw #LegalOps #FutureOfLaw

Who pays when AI agents mess up?

Picture hiring someone to run parts of your business, but the contract says nothing about who pays if they break something expensive. That's where AI agents sit right now. These systems can handle tasks like processing orders, managing schedules, and responding to customers without human oversight, but legal experts warn that liability rules remain murky when things go wrong. Companies are deploying AI agents to cut costs and speed up operations, yet no clear framework exists for determining who bears responsibility when an automated decision causes financial damage or regulatory trouble.

About SEO Brand SEO Brand is a globally recognized, top rated client acquisition agency with nearly 20 years of proven results. We exist for one reason: to help our clients grow their customer base through search, paid media, and AI powered visibility. From specialty practices to Fortune 500 companies, the organizations we work with do not just grow. They dominate their markets. While SEO Brand serves clients across multiple industries, healthcare has emerged as one of our strongest and most proven verticals. We have built a meaningful portfolio of healthcare case studies spanning specialty practices, multi location physician groups, and regional health systems, and the results have been exceptional. Our healthcare work speaks for itself. Now we are ready to scale it. We are hiring one person to make that happen. The Opportunity One job. One metric. Grow the pipeline. This is a newly created role. SEO Brand has built its client base through inbound leads and referrals. We are now opening a dedicated offline channel focused entirely on the healthcare vertical, and we need someone with the right relationships and the sales instincts to drive it. Your job is not to close deals. We have a Director of Sales who does that exceptionally well. Your job is to generate qualified conversations with healthcare marketing and patient acquisition leaders who control digital budgets at their organizations. Every introduction you make, every qualified conversation you initiate, every warm handoff you deliver to our sales team is the entire job. We take it from there. Healthcare clients in this space typically sign twelve month contracts and maintain those relationships well beyond that. You open the door once. The relationship sustains itself for years. Your commission is front loaded and paid at the point of conversion. You keep what you bring in and you are immediately back to hunting the next opportunity. The Relationships That Matter in This Role This is the most important section of this posting. Read it carefully before applying. Not every healthcare network is the right fit. The decision makers we need access to are the people who own marketing strategy, patient acquisition budgets, and digital vendor relationships at their organizations. Those titles look like: • Chief Marketing Officers and VPs of Marketing at hospitals, health systems, and large practices • Directors of Digital Marketing, Patient Experience, and Marketing Strategy • Directors of Marketing at specialty practices, multi location physician groups, and outpatient networks • Healthcare growth and strategy executives who own or directly influence marketing vendor decisions If your network inside healthcare is primarily clinical, meaning physicians, department heads, directors of procurement, or operations leadership, this role is not the right fit. #hiring CI/CD Engineer

Apply here: https://lnkd.in/giFtvxAk

Als CI/CD Engineer bij Conclusion Xforce werk je aan het bouwen en beheren van moderne gestandaardiseerde deployment pipelines in enterprise omgevingen. Je verbetert de snelheid en betrouwbaarheid van softwarelevering en integreert security- en compliance tooling bij klanten zoals Schiphol, NS en Rabobank.  Jouw rol In deze rol bouw en beheer je herbruikbare en schaalbare CI/CD pipelines binnen complexe omgevingen. Je optimaliseert bestaande workflows voor snelheid betrouwbaarheid en veiligheid en werkt met tooling zoals Azure DevOps GitLab GitHub Actions of Jenkins. Je integreert security- en compliance controles zoals SAST DAST en dependency scans en implementeert secrets-management met tools zoals HashiCorp Vault en Azure Key Vault. Je ondersteunt development teams met adoptie van CI/CD best practices implementeert GitOps workflows en artifact management en helpt bij troubleshooting en performance optimalisatie. Je werkt hands-on maar houdt ook het overzicht en draagt bij aan kennisdeling binnen het team. Je bent onderdeel van een no-nonsense cultuur waarin samenwerking met collegas belangrijk is en je oplossingen hebt die echt waarde toevoegen voor de klant.
